thoughts_bootstrapcommand__clijs_8113648.content: `parseProviderModel` requires the `provider/model` format and throws on bare model names like `MiniMax-M1` — needs a guard or format normalization upstream.

thoughts_parseprovidermodel_utility_8113.content: `parseProviderModel` throws (not a graceful error) when the model string doesn't match `provider/model` — callers must handle this.

facts_cli_argument_passthrough_811367688.content: CLI handlers can silently drop flags not explicitly forwarded to sub-commands — always audit the options object passed to the inner command.

facts_kodyadeengine_dist_patching_811368.content: The fix targets `dist/bin/cli.js` (the published build) rather than source, so no TypeScript source changes are needed — a valid approach for hotfixing published packages.

conventions_model_string_format_81137039.content: Bare model names without a provider prefix are not supported — the codebase expects `provider/model` format from CLI input.

thoughts_bootstrap_cli_1040406052393.content: The fix targets node_modules/@kody-ade/engine/dist/bin/cli.js — the published build, not source. Local source changes won't take effect unless rebuilt and republished.

facts_bootstrapcommand_model_parsing_104.content: The model-resolution helper parseProviderModel expects 'provider/model' format (e.g. 'minimax/MiniMax-M1'). If model and provider come in separately, they must be combined before calling it.

facts_cli_argument_passthrough_104040945.content: The --provider flag was parsed from CLI args but silently dropped before being forwarded to bootstrapCommand — a classic argument-tunneling bug.

thoughts_bootstrap_model_flag_behavior_1.content: The --model flag alone works because it encodes the provider as a prefix; --provider only matters when used alongside --model without the prefix.

thoughts_srcserverpayload_1238062825057.content: The CLI handler parsed --provider but never passed it in the options object to bootstrapCommand, silently dropping it downstream.

conventions_srcserverpayload_12380641927.content: parseProviderModel expects 'provider/model' format (e.g. 'minimax/MiniMax-M1'), not separate provider and model args.

facts_srcserverpayload_1238065927925.content: Minimal 2-line fix: pass provider in the CLI handler options and combine provider+model into the required 'provider/model' string.
